name: Miyan Ridi (Sesame Seed Soup)
intro: A rich, nutty and nutritious soup from the middle belt and northern part of Nigeria. It is also known as Beniseed Soup and has a similar appearance to Efo Egusi thanks to the ground sesame seeds.

ingredients.item: red bell peppers (deseeded and roughly chopped)
vegetable stock cubes Salt to taste

method: Add the bell peppers, tomatoes and scotch bonnet chillis and water to the blender and blend until smooth. Set aside.
Heat the palm in a pot, add the chopped onions and cook over medium-high heat for 2-3 minutes until the onions start to soften. Add the blended pepper mix and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the blended sesame seeds, along with the vegetable stock cubes and salt to taste. Stir well and add 1 cup of water. Simmer on low-medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chopped spinach and cook for a further 3 minutes.
